title: ‘Nine amazing years’: Jayson Tatum on his relationship with Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum described his nine years playing with Jaylen Brown as 'amazing,' despite recent trade to the 76ers.
Tatum and Brown won an NBA championship in 2024, with Tatum shocked by Brown's trade last week.
Celtics traded Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and draft picks due to new salary cap rules.

title: 4 standout moments from Tom Brady’s appearance on New Heights
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady discussed his 'welcome to the NFL moment' during a podcast appearance with Travis and Jason Kelce.
Brady highlighted a 2007 play with Randy Moss as a pivotal moment signaling a standout season.
Brady described the Belichick vs. Brady debate as 'dumb' and emphasized the importance of team collaboration.

title: France vs. Morocco World Cup tickets: Here’s how much last-minute seats cost at Boston Stadium
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: France and Morocco to compete in a World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium, Foxborough, on July 9, 2026.
Cheapest last-minute tickets range from $2,182.48 on Vivid Seats to $3,290 on FIFA Last-Minute Sales.
France ranks No. 1 and Morocco No. 6 in FIFA rankings for this anticipated match.

title: Brothers, town employees, allegedly attacked homeless man in Holbrook and broke several of his bones
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brothers Jesse and Jake Baxter are charged with felony assault after allegedly attacking a homeless man in Holbrook on June 13.
The victim, found bloodied and with broken bones, was reportedly attacked while sleeping near Brookville Park, according to police.
Court documents suggest the brothers may have mistaken the man for someone who argued with their father days earlier.

title: ‘It’s about my life’: Why acclaimed Cambridge wine bar Dear Annie is closing
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Dear Annie, a Cambridge wine bar, will end regular service July 31 to focus on private and bespoke wine events.
Owner Lauren Friel cited personal reasons, including motherhood, as key factors in deciding to change Dear Annie's format.
Dear Annie, opened in 2021, gained acclaim, featuring on America’s 50 favorite restaurants by The New York Times.

title: Here’s the Celtics’ 2026 Summer League roster and schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics' Summer League roster includes Hugo Gonzalez and first-round pick Chris Cenac Jr.
The Summer League schedule features games against Raptors, Hornets, Hawks, and Kings at Cox Pavilion.
Amile Jefferson is listed as the coach for the Boston Celtics Summer League team.

title: Tartan Army makes a big donation to help Mass. students in need
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Tartan Army donated $15,000 to the Massachusetts Child charity for students in need after their World Cup visit.
The donation was funded by savings from using school buses for transportation to matches at Gillette Stadium.
The funds will help students experiencing economic hardship purchase clothing, books, and school supplies, said the Massachusetts Teachers Association.

title: Your neighborhood Cumbies could soon look a little different
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cumberland Farms' parent company, EG Group, filed to go public, aiming to renovate and expand the chain nationwide.
Plans include refreshing 60 Cumberland Farm sites annually and rebranding 600-700 non-Cumberland sites within five years.
Cumberland, acquired in 2019, operates 1,500 locations with a net loss of $145 million on $16.34 billion revenue last year.

title: Boston sues social media companies over ‘addictive’ features, joining nationwide litigation
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu announced a lawsuit against Meta, TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube over targeting minors with addictive features.
The lawsuit, filed in California, is part of over 1,500 similar cases nationwide demanding removal of addictive features.
Boston Public Schools report increased mental health issues among students, with resources expanded to include 240 social workers and 105 psychologists.

title: Cyclospora, the explosive diarrhea parasite, has reached Mass. Here’s what to know.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts has confirmed 18 cases of cyclosporiasis since May 1, according to the Department of Public Health.
The CDC has reported 145 cases of cyclosporiasis nationwide, excluding a significant outbreak in Michigan.
Cyclospora is spread through contaminated food or water and symptoms include diarrhea, cramping, and nausea.
